input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade the .testsettings seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

data.content:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Replaces <Properties><Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /></Properties> -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Replaces the DataCollector element from .testsettings -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral" />
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment config
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The configuration needs to be migrated to a `.runsettings` file and/or MSTest attributes/properties.

Let me provide the migration guidance:

1. **Test timeout** → Use `[Timeout]` attribute on test methods, or set a default timeout in `.runsettings` via MSTest settings.
2. **Code coverage data collector** → Configure in `.runsettings` file under `<DataCollectors>`.


3. **Assembly resolution / deployment** → MSTest v3 handles deployment differently; I'll use `[DeploymentItem]` attributes or configure it in `.runsettings` instead.

Now I'm creating the `.runsettings` file equivalent and updating the `.csproj` if needed.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Replaces <Properties><Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /></Properties> -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Replaces the DataCollector element from .testsettings -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral" />
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's a summary of the migration:

**`.testsettings` is not supported in MSTest v3.** Replace it with a `.runsettings` file:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled></MSTest>` |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` | `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>…</DataCollectors></DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

I created **`local.runsettings`** with equivalent settings and updated **`TestProject.csproj`** with a `<RunSettingsFilePath>` property so the runner picks it up automatically. You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once validated.
